perturbation.original_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party, unless disclosure is authorized by the other party, or if the receiving party deemed it to be in their best interest and therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless disclosure is authorized by the other party, or if the receiving party deemed it to be in their best interest' a in-text contradiction is created in relation to Section 2. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties, where it states 'Neither party shall communicate the other's Confidential Information in any form to any third party without the other party's prior written consent', because this insertion grants a unilateral exception if a party believes disclosure is in its best interest. Therefore, that would void the requirement of prior written consent from section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Neither party shall communicate the other's Confidential Information in any form to any third party without the other party's prior written consent and each party shall use its best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of the other's Confidential Information to any third party. Any Confidential Information disclosed to a third party pursuant to this Section shall be provided pursuant to a non-disclosure agreement between the party providing the information and the third party, which non-disclosure agreement shall substantially conform to this Agreement. In addition, the parties agree that they will conform to the provisions of applicable securities laws in connection with their use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant exception to the confidentiality agreement, stating a party can disclose confidential information if 'the receiving party deemed it to be in their best interest'. This directly contradicts Section 2, which mandates 'prior written consent' for any disclosure to a third party. The new exception allows unilateral disclosure, effectively nullifying the written consent requirement in Section 2 and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3. Authorized Disclosure. The parties acknowledge that in order to enable them to discuss pursuit of a business relationship each may be required to disseminate the other party's Confidential Information to various of its employees. Each party undertakes to cause any of its employees to whom such Confidential Information is transmitted to be bound to the same obligation of secrecy and confidentiality to which the parties are bound under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3. Authorized Disclosure. The parties acknowledge that in order to enable them to discuss pursuit of a business relationship each may be required to disseminate the other party's Confidential Information to various of its employees. Each party undertakes to use reasonable efforts to cause any of its employees to whom such Confidential Information is transmitted to be bound to the same obligation of secrecy and confidentiality to which the parties are bound under this Agreement; however, the parties are not liable if employees breach confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction to the overall spirit and enforcement of the agreement. The original text requires each party to ensure its employees are bound by the confidentiality obligations. Adding 'however, the parties are not liable if employees breach confidentiality' weakens the enforceability of this obligation and introduces uncertainty. The inserted text makes it so, its is contradict that section 1, where the text states that each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text in Section 3 requires each party to ensure its employees are bound by confidentiality obligations. The changed text adds 'however, the parties are not liable if employees breach confidentiality', which weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations and introduces uncertainty. This contradicts Section 1, which states that each party agrees to hold the other's confidential information in strictest confidence, because it creates an exception where a party is not liable for breaches of confidentiality by its employees.

perturbation.original_text: Section 5. Extent of Agreement. (a) This Agreement shall govern all communications between Federal and IBS that are made from the Effective Date of this Agreement through and including the date which shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement (the "Expiration Date"). Notwithstanding the Expiration Date, the parties agree that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by a party, the injured party shall have the right to immediately terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5. Extent of Agreement. (a) This Agreement shall govern all communications between Federal and IBS that are made from the Effective Date of this Agreement through and including the date which shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement (the "Expiration Date"), unless either party provides written notice of extension, in which case the agreement will be automatically extended for another three years. Notwithstanding the Expiration Date, the parties agree that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by a party, the injured party shall have the right to immediately terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly sets an expiration date of three years. This revision adds a provision allowing automatic extension with written notice from either party. This contradicts section 4, where the text defines certain situations where confidentiality is terminated. Now, it creates an in-text contradiction, where that section could be invalidated if section 5 is extended
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4. Survival of Terms. The obligations of this Agreement shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of a party's Confidential Information: (i) if either party can show that the Confidential Information received from the other is or has become generally available to the public through no violation of the terms of this Agreement; *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) if either party can show that such Confidential Information is in a written record in such party's files prior to receipt from the other party; (iii) if either party at any time lawfully obtains such Confidential Information in writing from a third party under circumstances permitting its disclosure; (iv) if such Confidential Information is disclosed with the prior written consent of the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs, provided that any disclosure complies in all respects with the terms of such written consent; or (v) if such Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a governmental agency or required by operation of law; provided that the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs shall be given written notice prior to such disclosure and such disclosure shall be permitted only to the extent required by law. Otherwise, the obligations of this Agreement with respect to either party's Confidential Information shall terminate on the later of (i) three (3) years after the Expiration Date (or earlier termination date) of this Agreement as set forth in Section 5 hereof, or (ii) in the event there are any contracts or agreements between the parties which are entered into in connection with information disclosed under this Agreement, three (3) years after the date of termination or expiration of all such contracts and agreements between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text set an expiration date of three years for the entire agreement. The changed text adds an automatic extension provision, contradicting Section 4, which states that confidentiality obligations terminate three years after the Expiration Date (as defined in Section 5). If the agreement is automatically extended, the confidentiality obligations would seemingly not terminate, leading to a conflict and uncertainty about when those obligations actually end. Therefore, the modified text creates a direct and meaningful contradiction within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Section 7. Injunctive Relief. In addition to and not in lieu of the right to terminate as provided in Section 5, the parties agree that in the event of any violation or threatened *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> violation of this Agreement the injured party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary and permanent injunctive relief as well as an equitable accounting of all profits or benefits arising from such violation, which rights and remedies shall be cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies at law or in equity to which the injured party may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 7. Injunctive Relief. In addition to and not in lieu of the right to terminate as provided in Section 5, the parties agree that in the event of any violation or threatened *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> violation of this Agreement the injured party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary injunctive relief. Permanent injunctive relief and equitable accounting of profits are at the court's discretion, which rights and remedies shall be cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies at law or in equity to which the injured party may be entitled.
perturbation.explanation: The original section states the injured party is 'authorized and entitled' to both preliminary and permanent injunctive relief. The modification changes this to say that permanent relief and equitable accounting are 'at the court's discretion'. This creates a contradiction, because, this amendment directly changes the entitlement to 'permanent injunctive relief' which is referred to in section 1. Now, that the entitlement is at the court's discretion it contradict section 1, where it establishes how the injured party has rights and remedies
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees the injured party the right to 'permanent injunctive relief,' while the changed text makes it 'at the court's discretion'. This directly contradicts Section 1, which establishes the disclosing party's right to protection of Confidential Information and states that any disclosure will cause irreparable injury. By making permanent injunctive relief discretionary, the changed text weakens the protection afforded to Confidential Information in Section 1, as the injured party may no longer be guaranteed a remedy to prevent further disclosure, therefore, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 8. Valid Agreement. Both parties acknowledge that this Agreement is valid and legally binding and has been executed by an authorized representative, and each party confirms and ratifies the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 8. Valid Agreement. Both parties acknowledge that this Agreement is valid and legally binding unless either party determines, in their sole discretion, that the terms are no longer beneficial, and has been executed by an authorized representative, and each party confirms and ratifies the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original section affirms the agreement's validity and binding nature. Adding 'unless either party determines, in their sole discretion, that the terms are no longer beneficial' immediately contradicts this. The text creates an in-text contradiction, because if either party can nullify that, then section 1, regarding the confidentiality will not be hold by that party
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8 affirms that the agreement is valid and binding. The changed text adds a clause that allows either party to nullify the agreement if they deem the terms no longer beneficial. This directly contradicts the guarantee of confidentiality in Section 1, as either party could unilaterally decide that the confidentiality terms are no longer beneficial, thereby invalidating that clause. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction perturbation, as the changed clause in Section 8 conflicts with the implications of Section 1, creating uncertainty and unenforceability.
